note: Phase 1 seed. Shellcheck codes are SC2xxx (shell issues) and SC1xxx (parser issues). Most shell bugs live in the correctness lane — quoting, splitting, misuse of expansion. Naming/testability are sparse for shell; complexity is approximated via shellmetrics.

correctness_m1.description: Runtime-failure candidates in shell — unquoted expansions, subshell-scope confusion, array misuse. Routed to M1 / M5 (dry-run in bounded subprocess).

idiom_m7.description: Shell idiom / modernization — use of $() over backticks, [[ ]] over [ ], read -r. Routed to M7 (Idiom-fit).

complexity_m7.description: Function length / nesting depth via shellmetrics. Routed to M7 (Simplicity). Phase 1 seed; shellcheck itself has no complexity cops.

naming_m7.description: Variable naming — uppercase env vs. lowercase local. Routed to M7 (Clarity). Phase 1 seed.

testability_m7.description: Bats / shunit2 test-structure signals. Routed to M7 (Testability). Phase 1 seed.

security_defer_to_reaper.description: Shellcheck codes with security framing (unquoted user input, eval, insecure temp file) — Reaper R3 owns these (CWE lane). Listed for provenance; never mapped.
